doctor_summary:
The patient has active diabetes, with an HbA1c of 8.1% and recent glucose values of 138 mg/dL and 150 mg/dL. This indicates poor long-term glycemic control and persistent hyperglycemia despite treatment with insulin nph & regular human. 
Concurrently, the patient is experiencing hypertensive urgency, with a recorded blood pressure of 200/103 mmHg. This poses a significant risk for acute end-organ damage. The patient is currently on multiple antihypertensive medications, including atenolol, clonidine, furosemide, hydralazine, hydrochlorothiazide, lisinopril, and nifedipine, suggesting resistant or difficult-to-control hypertension.
patient_summary: Your blood sugar levels are currently high, with an A1c test showing 8.1% and recent glucose readings of 138 and 150. You are taking insulin to help with your blood sugar. Your blood pressure is also very high, measured at 200/103. You are taking several medications for your blood pressure. These high levels for both your blood sugar and blood pressure need to be reviewed by your doctor to help you stay healthy and prevent potential future problems.
